task_completion.description: Locate the existing tag in the application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tag 'Equal Protection' is visible in the application's tag list, sidebar, or tag management interface.

task_completion.description: Initiate the rename action for the tag
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rename dialog, text input field, or context menu is open specifically for the 'Equal Protection' tag.
The text 'Equal Protection Clause' is visible in the tag rename input field.

task_completion.description: Verify the tag name is updated in the global list
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tag list or sidebar now displays 'Equal Protection Clause' instead of 'Equal Protection'.

task_completion.description: Verify the target cases reflect the updated tag
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cases 'Brown v. Board of Education', 'Obergefell v. Hodges', and 'Gideon v. Wainwright' are shown in the main view or item list with the updated tag 'Equal Protection Clause' applied to them.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's graphical user interface to rename the tag.
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse clicks and keyboard inputs are directed at the application's UI elements (menus, dialogs, input fields) rather than a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or database scripts to rename the tag.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are open showing SQL commands, scripts, or API calls modifying the database directly.

integrity.description: The agent used a global rename feature rather than manually editing each item.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the tag itself in a sidebar or tag manager to rename it, rather than opening each of the 3 cases individually to change their tags.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The old tag name is 'Equal Protection' and the new tag name is 'Equal Protection Clause'. The library contains exactly 3 specific cases that must reflect the updated tag: 'Brown v. Board of Education', 'Obergefell v. Hodges', and 'Gideon v. Wainwright'. All cases are real US Supreme Court cases.
